notes: Each entry describes one vendored competitor subject under benches/corpora/<tool>/<subject>.
included=false entries are intentionally explicit so benchmark subsets cannot be silent.
golden=exact_examples is a fast deterministic smoke oracle over the published positive examples; full external-program oracle subjects remain listed with exclusion reasons.

corpus.subject: bc-example
corpus.exclude_reason: Requires the external bc oracle rather than a vendored A1 golden grammar; retained in the manifest for the full competitor run.

corpus.subject: lark-examples
corpus.exclude_reason: Grammar-only reference fixtures; no published positive example set is present under this subject.

corpus.subject: flex
corpus.exclude_reason: Requires the flex executable oracle and full GLADE protocol; retained as a vendored subject but excluded from the fast CI smoke gate.

corpus.subject: python
corpus.exclude_reason: Requires the Python interpreter oracle and a long-running full protocol; excluded from the always-on smoke gate with this explicit reason.

corpus.subject: python_wrapped
corpus.exclude_reason: Requires the wrapped Python interpreter oracle and a long-running full protocol; excluded from the always-on smoke gate with this explicit reason.

corpus.subject: sed
corpus.exclude_reason: Requires the sed executable oracle and full GLADE protocol; retained as a vendored subject but excluded from the fast CI smoke gate.

corpus.subject: xml
corpus.exclude_reason: Requires an XML parser oracle and the full GLADE protocol; retained as a vendored subject but excluded from the fast CI smoke gate.

corpus.subject: bc-example
corpus.exclude_reason: Requires the external bc oracle rather than a vendored A1 golden grammar; retained in the manifest for the full competitor run.
